emotional_core: The friction between raw data and real insight is where most data work actually happens — Pandas is the tool that makes that friction manageable instead of defeating.
visual_metaphor: An archaeologist at a dig site — layers of sediment representing messy data, each carefully chipped away to reveal structured information beneath. The tools are precise, the work is slow, but the artifact is worth finding.
key_quotes: Data cleaning is not preparation for analysis. It is analysis. You can't separate the two.
The raw data doesn't tell you anything. Summaries do.
Pandas doesn't replace thinking. It removes the mechanical friction that gets between you and thinking.

story_frames.visual: Close-up of a chaotic spreadsheet on screen — mismatched date formats, 'N/A' and 'na' and 'None' visible in same column, highlighted in red
story_frames.text: Two weeks. One dataset. Zero analysis.

story_frames.visual: Terminal window showing df.isnull().sum() output — several columns lit up with high null counts, cursor blinking
story_frames.text: Know your nulls before you start.

story_frames.visual: Side-by-side comparison: left panel shows raw string 'approx 50k', right panel shows NaN after pd.to_numeric(errors='coerce')
story_frames.text: Bad values become NaN. Not errors.

story_frames.visual: Clean Python terminal showing .query() syntax — readable, minimal, elegant compared to stacked boolean mess
groupby().agg() output rendered as a clean DataFrame — regions as rows, named metrics as columns, all aligned
Pivot table output — regions on rows, months on columns, revenue values filling the grid cleanly
Person leaning back from laptop, looking at clean visualization on screen — relief and focus simultaneously
